svg.text: Control Plane uses a separate read-only core pool
Go (chi, pgx), Docker. REST API + XML renderer
+ ESL client + audit + CDR ingest/query + live
+ phone provisioning + voicemail read API + call-
audio + video; served by Docker Compose
Reads all 3 PostgreSQL DBs read-only via grafana_ro
Runs in the same Docker Compose
Docker Compose runs postgres + control-plane + webphone + grafana. Control Plane publishes
Fetches directory + dialplan (live). Control Plane can
Posts a JSON CDR to the Control Plane
Runtime state in PostgreSQL via ODBC
Voicemail; message store in PostgreSQL freeswitch_core
Mailbox params vm-* come from directory
Control Plane read-only pool to freeswitch_core
Control Plane proxies recording list + download
• E3 /xml/*: TLS + mutual TLS + HTTP Basic auth; directory returns a1-hash, never plaintext SIP password.
• E5 SIP: digest auth; FreeSWITCH validates against a1-hash from E3.
• TLS material: self-signed CA; server cert for Control Plane SAN includes 172.31.30.216 / 127.0.0.1 /
localhost; client cert for FreeSWITCH; audit logs redact passwords.
• E7 ODBC: PostgreSQL user/password; FreeSWITCH runtime DBs (no sqlite).
• E8 WSS: TLS (our CA) on 7443 for WebRTC.
• E11 CDR: POST /cdr is FreeSWITCH-facing → same mTLS + Basic auth as /xml/*.
• E12 events: SSE requires Bearer token; wallboard passes it via fetch.
• E13 provisioning: Basic auth + CIDR allowlist; configs carry cleartext SIP password by nature.
• Grafana: read-only grafana_ro DB role; writes denied on all 3 DBs.
• Voicemail: voicemail.password is redacted in audit log and never appears in directory XML; only vm-
1. Declarative config change: Operator → Terraform → Control Plane → PostgreSQL; Terraform → POST /runtime/reloadxml → Control
2. SIP registration: Softphone/WebRTC → FreeSWITCH; FreeSWITCH → /xml/directory → Control Plane → PostgreSQL; a1-hash returned and
3. Call into an IVR: dial 3333 → FreeSWITCH; /xml/dialplan(context=company) → Control Plane → PostgreSQL; answer → Piper TTS /
4. Call center: dial 4444 → mod_callcenter(support@…) → MOH → Available agent rings; runtime + record-template recording; all
5. Conference: dial 3500 or 3501 + PIN → mod_conference video grid; profiles from configuration binding.
6. CDR: call ends → mod_json_cdr POSTs JSON → Control Plane /cdr → parsed → stored in PostgreSQL → queried via GET /api/v1/cdr and
7. Live events: FreeSWITCH events → ESL listener (Control Plane) → in-process hub → GET /api/v1/events (SSE) → supervisor
8. Phone provisioning: phone boots → GET /provision/<mac> → Control Plane looks up provisioned_devices → renders vendor config
with SIP password pulled from directory user.
9. Voicemail: caller leaves a message → mod_voicemail stores it in freeswitch_core (ODBC); vm-* comes from directory; read API
returns list + unread (MWI) via read-only core pool.
• Call center: queue support@192.168.48.143, entry 4444, agents 4201/4202
• API surface: audit log, list pagination, CDR (/api/v1/cdr + /cdr/stats), live
• Observability: Grafana NOC dashboard on :3000 (read-only grafana_ro).
freeswitch_device resource; voicemail = {} block on freeswitch_user;

Notes: All services run in Docker on Zone A (postgres + control-plane + webphone + grafana). Control Plane publishes :8080, webphone :8443, Grafana :3000; Postgres :5432 for local use. FreeSWITCH on Zone B keeps NO
sqlite — its core db, sofia registrations, mod_callcenter and fifo/db/voicemail all live in PostgreSQL via ODBC (E7), so the FS host depends on the dev-box Postgres being reachable at boot.
